beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and get ready for the day at home.
beats.reasoning: Aiden is a night owl but can't start the day without a swim, so early wake-up is non-negotiable. Low energy (40/100) from yesterday means a quick, efficient morning routine.

beats.activity: Swim at the IMA pool for the morning ritual.
beats.reasoning: Non-negotiable start to Aiden's day. Despite rain beginning at 7am, the indoor pool is unaffected. This restores energy and sets the tone.

beats.activity: Commute to the warehouse in the U-District.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closure (9am–noon) creates backup; Aiden leaves early to beat rush and avoid the bridge closure entirely by staying on the Seattle side.

beats.activity: Work at the warehouse: process shipments and coordinate logistics.
beats.reasoning: Morning shift during the SR-520 closure window. Steady drizzle continues; indoor work is ideal. Yesterday's backlog momentum carries over.

beats.activity: Grab lunch at a nearby café, staying dry indoors.
beats.reasoning: Rain is steady until 2pm; Aiden stays indoors for lunch. Low energy and drizzle make a quick, local meal preferable to exploring far.

beats.activity: Afternoon work shift: continue logistics coordination and shipment processing.
beats.reasoning: Rain clears by 2pm; Aiden stays focused on work through the afternoon. Spontaneous energy is low, so staying put is natural.

beats.activity: Commute home to Redmond, rain now cleared.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure is over; rain has stopped. Aiden can take the bridge now. Budget extra time for residual evening traffic.

beats.activity: Stop at Safeway to grab groceries and supplies for urban gardening.
beats.reasoning: Evening is now clear; Aiden picks up supplies on the way home. Urban gardening is a key interest, and evening light is still decent for planning tomorrow's plants.

beats.activity: Work on urban garden plots and container plants at home.; Paint and sketch in the studio space at home, then light dinner.
beats.reasoning: After a full work day, Aiden unwinds with hands-on gardening. Low energy suggests quiet, solo activity rather than socializing. This is wind-down time.
Aiden settles in for 8+ hours of rest, spanning midnight. Night owl means later bedtime is natural. Ends before 6:30am to allow early swim tomorrow.

diary: Woke up early and hit the IMA pool before work—needed that swim to clear my head. The rain was coming down hard when I got to the warehouse, but it kept things quiet. Processed shipments all morning while the SR-520 closure messed up traffic everywhere. Grabbed lunch at Café Solstice around noon; their espresso hit different today. Afternoon flew by with more logistics coordination. By the time I left, the rain had stopped and the commute home was smooth. Picked up some soil and new seedlings at Safeway, then spent a good couple hours tending the garden plots. The containers are really coming in now—basil's getting thick. Sketched a bit in the studio after, nothing serious. Just felt good to have my hands in dirt and paint today. There's something grounding about it.
thought_process: The early swim felt essential—I knew the day would be desk-heavy, so I prioritized that movement first thing. The rain and SR-520 closure created this weird calm at work; fewer shipments, fewer interruptions. I stayed put at the warehouse instead of risking the traffic chaos. By afternoon, I was itching to get home and work with my hands. Grabbed supplies specifically because I'd been thinking about the garden all week. The shift from logistics coordination to actual gardening felt like moving between two versions of myself—one managing systems, one creating something. Painting after helped me decompress. The weather clearing up felt symbolic somehow, like permission to relax.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
